title: single-name-vs-sector output
description: Canonical structured output for the single-name-vs-sector skill. Layer 1 (the data); Layer 2 (rendered RS table + divergence block + take) is described in references/rendering.md.

as_of.description: Local as-of date the run was anchored to.

fetched_at.description: UTC timestamp when the script ran.

ticker.description: The single name measured against its sector.

sector.description: SPDR sector ETF the name is mapped to (or --sector override).

sector_label.description: Human-readable sector name for the ETF.

benchmark.description: Benchmark for the sector-vs-market leg. Default SPY.

windows_days.description: Lookback windows in trading days for returns and RS.

name_return_by_window.description: Name total return (decimal) keyed `{w}d_pct`. Null when history is insufficient.

name_vs_sector_bps.description: (name_return - sector_return) * 10000, keyed `{w}d_bps`. Null when either leg lacks history.

n_obs_per_window.description: Bars available for the name per window, keyed `{w}d`.

trend_name_vs_sector.description: Five-bucket trend of the name-vs-sector RS across windows.

trend_sector_vs_benchmark.description: Five-bucket trend of the sector-vs-benchmark RS across windows.

trend_name_vs_benchmark.description: Five-bucket trend of the name-vs-benchmark RS across windows.

divergence.description: Summary of how far the name is running from its own sector.

score_bps.description: Name-vs-sector RS averaged across windows (signed). Positive = name leading its sector, negative = lagging.

composite_bps.description: Mean of the absolute name-vs-sector RS across windows: magnitude of the gap regardless of direction.

classification.description: diverging when the name-vs-sector move and the sector-vs-benchmark move point opposite ways; otherwise the sign of the name-vs-sector average.

take.description: One-sentence read: is the divergence name-specific or sector-driven, with the driving windows.

tier_caveats.description: Honest caveats: rate-limit flags, missing history, override notes, proxy limitations.
